title: Verific frontend does not maintain initial values
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Using SymbiYosys and Yosys compiled with the Verific frontend, run:
```
unzip example.zip
cd example
sby -f run.sby pdr
```

[example.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/2793093/example.zip)

The property fails because the `initstate` register does not start with the value 1.

If you modify the `[script]` section of `run.sby` to use `read_verilog` instead of `read`, and re-run it, the property is now proven.

## Expected behavior

I would expect that the Verific frontend gives the same verification result as the internal frontend. However, I'd be happy if there were just a good way of constraining the intial state through the Verific frontend. I haven't found a way to do that yet. Perhaps there's a command for this? I looked at the yosys help but didn't see anything.

## Actual behavior

Using the Verific frontend does not preserve the initial values of registers. I also tried using an `initial` block, which Verific ignores. Thus, the Verific frontend produces a different verification result than the regular frontend.

Thank you very much for your time and please let me know if I can provide any more information!
